div1.entryFree:
Broach, vb., 1) to spit: bringing rebellion --ed on his sword, H5 V Chor. H5 V Chor. I'll b. the tadpole on my rapier's point, Tit. IV, 2, 85.
2) to tap: if I would b. the vessels of my love, Tim. II, 2, 186. he bravely --ed his boiling bloody breast, Mids. V, 148.
3) to let out, to shed: this blow should b. thy dearest blood, H6A III, 4, 40. H6B IV, 10, 40. H6C II, 3, 16.
4) to set abroach (q. v.), to set loose, to begin: I will continue that I --ed in jest, Shr. I, 2, 84. a portent of --ed mischief to the unborn times, H4A V, 1, 21. what hath --ed this tumult but thy pride? H6C II, 2, 159. that for her love such quarrels may be --ed, Tit. II, 1, 67. the business she hath --ed in the state, Ant. I, 2, 178. Ant. I, 2, 178
Followed by to, == to give the first hint or impulse: whether ever I did b. this business to your majesty, H8 II, 4, 149.
